name: Bluescreen
description: An ancient spirit that haunts beige plastic towers and CRT monitors. It was born the first time a Windows 98 machine crashed during an important school presentation. It feeds on unsaved Word documents and dial-up connection timeouts. Its face IS the BSOD. It communicates by playing the Windows XP startup sound at 3am. Nobody knows why it still exists. It just does. It always will.

moves.name: 💙 Fatal Exception
moves.desc: Bluescreen fills the opponent's vision with white text on blue. A fatal exception OE has occurred at 0028:C0011E36. Press any key. There is no key. Deals heavy damage and confuses.

moves.name: 📞 Dial-Up Screech
moves.desc: Bluescreen connects to the internet at 56k. The sound it makes is ungodly. The opponent's speed crumbles as they wait 45 minutes for a JPEG to load top to bottom.

moves.name: 🔧 Defragment
moves.desc: Runs a full disk defragmentation. Takes an indeterminate amount of time. Nobody knows if it actually helps. Restores 25 HP. You cannot use the computer while this is happening.

moves.name: 📎 It Looks Like You're Losing
moves.desc: Clippy emerges from the void. 'It looks like you're losing a battle. Would you like help?' Bluescreen's ATK sharply rises while the opponent is distracted trying to close the dialogue box.

moves.name: 🌐 Internet Explorer
moves.desc: Opens Internet Explorer. That's it. That's the move. The sheer slowness of it lowers the opponent's defense as they fall asleep waiting for it to launch.

moves.name: 📄 Unsaved Document
moves.desc: Crashes mid-battle and takes the opponent's 10-page essay with it. The opponent is poisoned by pure existential dread AND confused trying to remember if AutoSave was on. It was not.
